title: Linux Forensics: RHEL Compromise — Live Bash
description: lnx-web-02 is calling out to TCP/4444 from inside the perimeter. SSH brute force success → REDACTED privesc → cron-based persistence → reverse shell. Walk /var/log/secure, /etc/cron.d, ps, ss, find -perm -4000.

hints.text: `connect lnx-web-02`. Start with `who` and `last` — there's a session from a non-corp IP that shouldn't be there.
`cat /var/log/secure | include Accepted` ties the foothold to a username and source IP. `cat /var/log/secure | include sudo` shows the next move.
`ls /etc/cron.d` — anything outside the stock RHEL set is suspect. `cat` the body and the referenced script.
